summary: Wired safe automatic repo-automation updates into Owlory's local validation and pre-push path. Added --auto-update safety checks, Make targets, pre-push manifest-change detection, docs, and unit tests. Synced the manifest-owned changes into the external repo and explicitly pushed external HEAD 7eb3beff41aad923122b8674cf0129ac48c0bf91.
validations_passed: git fetch origin main && git pull --rebase origin main
missing_proof_levels: Consumer adoption proof remains queued in repo-automation-consumer-adoption-smoke.
No real third-party repository migration was attempted.

contract_status_changes.before: Owlory had a tested sync/check tool, but normal validation and pre-push workflows did not update the external repo-automation target automatically.
contract_status_changes.after: .githooks/pre-push detects pushes touching manifest-owned reusable automation sources and runs make repo-automation-update, which syncs the external target and verifies check-mode passes.

contract_status_changes.before: The sync tool could create or overwrite a target through --sync, which is appropriate for bootstrap but too broad for normal pre-push automation.
contract_status_changes.after: --auto-update refuses missing targets, non-Git targets, and targets with local dirt before syncing; --sync remains available for explicit bootstrap work.

contract_status_changes.before: Remote publication existed, but automatic update behavior was not wired.
contract_status_changes.after: The hook updates local external contents only. External Git commit and remote push remain explicit operator actions; this slice performed that explicit sync/commit/push and external main is mirrored at 7eb3beff41aad923122b8674cf0129ac48c0bf91.

residual_risks: Pre-push detection is based on changed paths relative to the branch upstream; unusual push shapes may require running make repo-automation-update manually.
The consumer-adoption slice still needs to prove a non-Owlory repository can use the reusable package.
recommended_next_reason: The external package is now bootstrapped, published, and kept current by the Owlory automation path, so the next adjacent proof is a temp consumer-repository adoption smoke.
